Talent Intelligence Platform

Turning conference attendance into a queryable, explainable talent pool.

Oz Shwartz · Candidate Presentation · AI Solution Manager

Agenda

What we'll cover

  1. 1 The problem - why good candidates get lost
  2. 2 The solution & how scoring works
  3. 3 AI's role - explains, never decides
  4. 4 Live demo
  5. 5 Results - real numbers, not a pitch
  6. 6 What's built vs. what's next
  7. 7 Why it matters to HR
The Problem

Every conference we attend, we meet great people - and then lose them

  • Business cards and notes disappear. Nothing lands in a structured system.
  • When a new role opens, recruiters start from zero - even for people they've already met.
  • Not everyone at a conference is actually relevant to it. A DevOps conference draws platform engineers and recruiters, vendors, IT managers - attendance alone proves nothing.
The Solution

An always-on talent pool that tells you who's worth contacting - and why

Every conference contact becomes a searchable, scored, explainable candidate - automatically re-surfaced the moment a matching role opens.

Conference
→
Capture
→
Score
→
Match to Jobs
→
Recruiter Action
How It Works

Transparent scoring - not a black box

Every weight is live-editable in Settings - no hardcoded logic, no redeploy needed.

A domain-relevance gate dampens the score when a title reads as an adjacent, non-technical role - even if they attended the right conference. This is the signal-vs-noise mechanism.
AI's Role

AI explains. It never decides the score.

The deterministic engine is the single source of truth. AI narrates the result in plain language - and every AI output carries its own confidence score, so no claim is ever stated with more certainty than the data supports.

Runs fully on a deterministic fallback with zero LLM cost - and upgrades automatically to a real model the moment an API key is set. Nothing about the scoring changes either way.

Results

Proof, not a pitch

Running the pipeline against JOB001 (Senior ML Engineer) - 75 real conference attendees, scored automatically:

75
Candidates Evaluated
4
Strong Match
12
Potential Match
30
Do Not Contact
Lucas Evans Strong Match
75.8 / 100 · Senior ML Engineer at IMG Media · 8 years experience
System-generated: "Ask Maya Levi (Senior ML Engineer, AI/ML) for a warm introduction."
Scope

What's built vs. what's next

Built & working today
  • Full pipeline: repository → scoring engine → AI layer
  • FastAPI backend + complete React app, 5 pages
  • CSV import/export, job CRUD, smart referral suggestions
Deliberately deferred
  • Live LinkedIn / HubSpot / Comeet integrations
  • Auth & RBAC, CI/CD pipeline
  • Conference registration intake form
Why It Matters

For the Head of HR, not the engineer

  • Recruiters stop re-searching people they've already met.
  • Every conference becomes a measurable investment, not just a networking trip.
  • Every recommendation is explainable - never "the algorithm said so."
Summary

Three things to remember

  • Signal-to-noise is solved, and proven with real numbers - not a promise.
  • Deterministic scoring is the source of truth; AI only explains it.
  • Working end-to-end today: pipeline, API, and a full application.
